domain: Indonesian batik wax-resist dyeing: hot beeswax applied via canting pen or tjap stamp to PROTECT areas from dye; fabric dyed; wax boiled off after dyeing; multi-color via sequential wax+dye+remove cycles; negative-mask selective protection.
specific_mechanism: Negative-mask selective protection via removable physical layer; information lives in what is protected, not in what is added; sequential layering for multi-color output.
llm_application: BATIK-WAX-RESIST null-space negative-mask fine-tune: for each fine-tune task T, train small MASKING SUBNETWORK m_T(W) producing per-weight 0/1 protection mask. Update only UNPROTECTED weights: W_T = W + (1 - m_T(W)) ⊙ Δθ_T. Multi-task: sequentially apply T1, T2, ... each with its own learned mask; tasks never interfere because T1's protected region preserved during T2 if m_T2 also marks it as protected. Differs from (a) standard LoRA (low-rank not mask), (b) sparse fine-tuning (no learnable mask network), (c) PESO subspace, (d) DoRA, (e) LIFT (Principal Weights selection) by combining (i) LEARNABLE per-weight mask network + (ii) NEGATIVE-protect interpretation + (iii) SEQUENTIAL multi-task layering.
novelty_claim: Sparse fine-tuning selects params. LoRA uses low-rank. PESO subspace. DoRA direction-rank. LIFT Principal Weights. BATIK-WAX-RESIST recombines (a) LEARNABLE MASK SUBNETWORK + (b) NEGATIVE-protect + (c) SEQUENTIAL multi-task layering. Three known components combined; not wholly new.
content_words: canting pen tjap stamp wax application
